html:
Exclusive to:

The Lake is a feature exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. It is a large body of water that spawns in its own Desert Set Piece called the Oasis.

During any Seasons other than Summer, the Lake will be dry and unusable. Players can walk over it during this time.

During Summer, the Lake will fill up with water. By using a Fishing Rod, Freshwater Fish and Crumpled Packages can be caught at any time of Day. There are 15 Fish or Packages, and one is refilled every 90 seconds. The chance of getting a Fish is 40%, and getting a Package is 60%. Fishing takes 4 seconds, with 2.4 seconds added for every fish missing from the Lake.

Three Fireflies naturally spawn around the center of the Lake when it fills every Summer, illuminating it at night. They are not catchable while the Lake is full of water.

Contents

  • 1 Tips
  • 2 Trivia
  • 3 Bugs
  • 4 Gallery

Tips

  • Making a small outpost near the Oasis can be beneficial as players may need to stay at the Lake for long periods of time to fish out the various rare Blueprints only obtainable through Crumpled Packages.
  • The Oasis is also a viable place to build a Summer base, as plants and structures will not smolder and cause Wildfires throughout the entire surrounding Desert biome. This also gives players easy access to Cactus Flowers, Volt Goats and the Antlion, as long as they have obtained Desert Goggles to counter the constant Sandstorm.
  • The Lake may dry up after filling on the first day of Summer if it Rains. Once rain stops falling, this unexpected change is reversed, and more Succulents can be gathered.
  • The Lake will not fill up when something that isn't Fireflies is right on top of it, such as a player or an item that wasn't properly pushed away.

Trivia

  • The Lake was added in the Against The Grain update.
  • Items dropped on the dried Lake will be pushed off to the edge so that they may still be picked up after it fills with water.
  • The Lake is renewable source of Fireflies such as surrounding area of the Great Tree Trunks.
  • If the player begins fishing in the lake with zero fishes in it, the character will simply hold the rod indefinitely until the player cancels the action, even if the count of fishes becomes non-zero after the line has been cast.

Bugs

  • When the Lake fills up with water, some entities standing on it will persist doing so until they move away.

Gallery

  • The dry Lake in Autumn.
  • The wet Lake in Summer.
  • The wet Lake at night.
  • A bugged Crow on a wet Lake.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

"oasislake"
“It's dry as a bone.”
–Wilson, examining the dry lake.
“It's a bunch of dirt.”
–Willow, examining the dry lake.
“Lake should be here!”
–Wolfgang, examining the dry lake.
“If only my tears could dry up like this lake.”
–Wendy, examining the dry lake.
“DRY LAKES ARE FAR SUPERIOR TO WET ONES”
–WX-78, examining the dry lake.
“Perhaps it's not the right time of year.”
–Wickerbottom, examining the dry lake.
“There's nothin' but dirt here.”
–Woodie, examining the dry lake.
“It's not in season.”
–Maxwell, examining the dry lake.
“Naught but a barren basin.”
–Wigfrid, examining the dry lake.
“We can make mud pies!”
–Webber, examining the dry lake.
“There's nothing but mud.”
–Warly, examining the dry lake.
“Oh. Empty”
–Wormwood, examining the dry lake.
“There used to be water there.”
–Winona, examining the dry lake.
“No swimming today!”
–Wortox, examining the dry lake.
“Nooooo! Where water go?!”
–Wurt, examining the dry lake.
“An invisible lake?”
–Walter, examining the dry lake.
“There might've been water here once... or maybe there will be.”
–Wanda, examining the dry lake.
“Is that a mirage?”
–Wilson, examining the summer lake.
“It's a bunch of water.”
–Willow, examining the summer lake.
“Splishes and splashes.”
–Wolfgang, examining the summer lake.
“A place to drown my sorrows.”
–Wendy, examining the summer lake.
“EVEN DESERTS ARE NOT SAFE FROM WATER”
–WX-78, examining the summer lake.
“It could support a small ecosystem.”
–Wickerbottom, examining the summer lake.
“That's a whole lotta wet.”
–Woodie, examining the summer lake.
“Didn't think I'd ever come back here.”
–Maxwell, examining the summer lake.
“Perhaps a sword-wielding maiden lies beneath.”
–Wigfrid, examining the summer lake.
“That's a pretty lake!”
–Webber, examining the summer lake.
“I could use a little break.”
–Warly, examining the summer lake.
“Water! Water!”
–Wormwood, examining the summer lake.
“Never seen such a clear lake before.”
–Winona, examining the summer lake.
“Swimming gives me wet imp smell.”
–Wortox, examining the summer lake.
“Water!!”
–Wurt, examining the summer lake.
“We're saved, Woby!”
–Walter, examining the summer lake.
“Water! Finally!”
–Wanda, examining the summer lake.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Hồ là một tính năng độc quyền của Don't Starve Together, được giới thiệu trong A New Reign. Đó là một khối nước lớn sinh ra trong Sa mạc Set Piece của riêng nó được gọi là Ốc đảo.

The '''Lake''' is a feature exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[A New Reign]]''. It is a large body of water that spawns in its own [[Desert]] [[Set Piece#Oasis|Set Piece]] called the Oasis.

During any [[Season]]s other than [[Summer]], the Lake will be dry and unusable. Players can walk over it during this time.

During Summer, the Lake will fill up with water. By using a [[Fishing Rod]], [[Freshwater Fish]] and [[Crumpled Package]]s can be caught at any time of [[Day]]. There are 15 Fish or Packages, and one is refilled every 90 seconds. The chance of getting a Fish is 40%, and getting a Package is 60%. Fishing takes 4 seconds, with 2.4 seconds added for every fish missing from the Lake.  

Three [[Fireflies]] naturally spawn around the center of the Lake when it fills every Summer, illuminating it at night. They are not catchable while the Lake is full of water.

== {{pic|32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* Making a small outpost near the Oasis can be beneficial as players may need to stay at the Lake for long periods of time to fish out the various rare [[Blueprints]] only obtainable through Crumpled Packages.
* The Oasis is also a viable place to build a Summer base, as plants and structures will not smolder and cause [[Summer#Wildfires|Wildfires]] throughout the entire surrounding Desert biome. This also gives players easy access to [[Cactus Flower]]s, [[Volt Goat|Volt Goats]] and the [[Antlion]], as long as they have obtained [[Desert Goggles]] to counter the constant [[Sandstorm]].
*The Lake may dry up after filling on the first day of Summer if it [[Rain]]s. Once rain stops falling, this unexpected change is reversed, and more [[Petals#Succulent|Succulents]] can be gathered.
*The Lake will not fill up when something that isn't Fireflies is right on top of it, such as a player or an item that wasn't properly pushed away.

=={{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* The Lake was added in the ''[[A New Reign#February 2nd.2C_2017 - Against the Grain|Against The Grain]]'' update.
* Items dropped on the dried Lake will be pushed off to the edge so that they may still be picked up after it fills with water.
* The Lake is renewable source of [[Fireflies]] such as surrounding area of the [[Great Tree Trunk|Great Tree Trunks]].
*If the player begins fishing in the lake with zero fishes in it, the character will simply hold the rod indefinitely until the player cancels the action, even if the count of fishes becomes non-zero after the line has been cast.

=={{pic|32|Mosquito}} Bugs ==
* When the Lake fills up with water, some entities standing on it will persist doing so until they move away.

=={{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery ==
